content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify real, interconnected defects in the planned-elsewhere validation pipeline. The core issue: restart_required is computed from raw mapped_to_bead_id values before any validation occurs, the post-commit recorder skips validation when the allowed set is empty (which is the normal case when no PE items exist in the prompt), and the reconstruction path in success_reconciliation bypasses validation entirely. Together these create a path where a hallucinated-but-real bead ID can suppress the restart/fix loop.

## Votes

- `fr-17-fc75ff73`: Accept
  extract_pe_bead_ids is a critical parsing function that gates which bead IDs are accepted as planned-elsewhere mappings. It has non-trivial parsing logic (section header matching, bullet parsing, stopping at next heading) with no test coverage. A bug here could silently accept hallucinated bead IDs or reject legitimate ones, directly undermining the validation that amendments fr-17-59a68d4c and fr-17-1292e667 depend on. The proposed test cases (normal bullets, no PE section, empty section, multiple beads, heading boundary) are well-chosen and cover the realistic edge cases.
- `fr-17-59a68d4c`: Accept
  This identifies a real validation gap. The code at engine.rs:7641 explicitly skips validation when allowed_pe_ids is empty ('if !allowed_pe_ids.is_empty() && ...'), and the normal 'no PE items' prompt text (service.rs:1047-1050) produces prose with no bullets, yielding an empty set from extract_pe_bead_ids. This means any mapped_to_bead_id is silently accepted when no PE section was provided. More critically, the restart_required decision at final_review.rs:1569-1573 is computed from raw mapped_to_bead_id values BEFORE record_planned_elsewhere_amendments runs, so even if post-commit validation rejects the mapping, the restart has already been suppressed. Fail-closed behavior (treating empty allowed set as 'no valid PE mappings') is the correct fix — if no PE beads were listed in the prompt, no PE classification should be accepted.
- `fr-17-1292e667`: Accept
  This is a distinct and complementary defect to fr-17-59a68d4c. Even if the restart_required decision were fixed to validate before committing, reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings (success_reconciliation.rs:975-1024) reads directly from the committed aggregate payload and recreates PlannedElsewhereMapping entries without any validation against the allowed PE set. It only deduplicates against existing mappings (seen_keys check at line 992). This means an invalid PE classification that was committed in the aggregate can be reconstructed and persisted to milestone state during reconciliation, surviving even if the original record_planned_elsewhere_amendments call correctly rejected it. The fix must either strip/convert invalid PE classifications before the aggregate is committed, or add validation to the reconstruction path — ideally both, as defense in depth.
